DECLARATION OF LIDIYA SKLOVSKAYA IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS? REPLY TO OPPOSITION (TRANSACTION ID # 68417095) FILED BY DEFENDANT UNGER, IGOR SKLOVSKAYA, LIDIYA

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS IGOR UNGER AND LIDIYA SKLOVSKAYA'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN THEIR BURDEN UNDER CCP 473(B) OR CCP 473.5. DEFAULT WAS ENTERED ON APRIL 7, 2021 (MORE THAN 6 MONTHS BEFORE THE FILING OF THIS MOTION); NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT WAS SERVED ON MAY 20, 2022 (MORE THAN 120 DAYS BEFORE THE FILING OF THIS MOTION); REFERENCE TO RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(D) IS NOT SUPPORTED BY ARGUMENT OR EVIDENCE PRESENTED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANT FAILS TO SUSTAIN THEIR BURDEN TO SHOW LACK OF ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE LAWSUIT, MISTAKE, SURPRISE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER NEW ARGUMENTS AND EVIDENCE SUBMITTED IN REPLY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; COURT REPORTER: JAMIE DAVIS, CSR #14209, JAMIEDAVISCSR@GMAIL.COM. (D501)

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Cleve B. Collado; Clapp, Moroney, Vucinich, Beeman and Scheley, APC; 650-989-5400; appearing on behalf of defendants Igor Unger and Lidiya Sklovskaya.
Sydney Chase; Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc.; 415-771-9850; appearing on behalf of plaintiffs Theodore Scott-Smith and Michael Conant.

Court reporter: Jamie Davis, CSR #14209, jamiedavisCSR@gmail.com.

Defendants contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS IGOR UNGER AND LIDIYA SKLOVSKAYA'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain their burden under CCP 473(b) or CCP 473.5. Default was entered on April 7, 2021 (more than 6 months before the filing of this motion); Notice of Entry of Judgment was served on May 20, 2022 (more than 120 days before the filing of this motion); reference to relief under CCP 473(d) is not supported by argument or evidence presented with the moving papers. Additionally, Defendant fails to sustain their burden to show lack of actual notice of the lawsuit, mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. The Court will not consider new arguments and evidence submitted in reply.

Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Jamie Davis, CSR #14209, jamiedavisCSR@gmail.com. (D501)

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-14-2022 Continued To Nov-30-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-13-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-10-2022 Continued To Aug-03-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

UNCONTESTED
Off Calendar Apr-19-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendants Igor Unger and Lidiya Sklovskaya.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:58 a.m., adjourning at 11:25 a.m. and reconvening at 11:39 a.m.

Raquel Fox, Esq. and Sydney Chase, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Theodore Scott-Smith, who was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Fox and Ms. Chase appeared for plaintiff Michael Conant, who was not present.
Mr. Scott-Smith, along with Ms. Fox and Ms. Chase, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Igor Unger or Lidiya Sklovskaya.

Plaintiffs' Complaint for Damages and Injunctive Relief was filed on September 2, 2020, and was personally served on defendant Igor Unger on October 24, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiffs' Statement of Damages on defendant Igor Unger occurred on December 16, 2020, and was effectuated on December 26, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiffs' Summons, Complaint, Statement of Damages and accompanying documents on defendant Lidiya Sklovskaya occured on December 16, 2020, and was effectuated on December 26, 2020.
Defaults as to defendants Igor Unger and Lidiya Sklovskaya were entered on April 7, 2021.
DOE defendants were dismissed on September 23, 2021.
Order waiving the appearance of plaintiff Michael Conant at today's hearing was filed on March 7, 2022.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Theodore Scott-Smith and Michael Conant, and against the defaulted defendants Igor Unger and Lidiya Sklovskaya, as follows:

PLAINTIFF THEODORE SCOTT-SMITH:
Compensatory Damages - $79,200.00.
Emotional Distress Damages - $50,000.00.
Punitive Damages - $200,000.00.
Sub-Total - $329,200.00.

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL CONANT:
Compensatory Damages - $28,800.00.
Emotional Distress Damages - $50,000.00.
Punitive Damages - $200,000.00.
Sub-Total - $278,800.00.

Attorney Fees - $16,832.50.
Costs - $1,445.11.

Total - $626,277.61.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibit.

Proceedings concluded at 11:48 a.m.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Continued To Apr-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ms. Sydney Chase appearing for Plaintiff Michael Conant who is excused by waiver, and Plaintiff Theodore Scott-Smith who is also appearing by Zoom placed under oath and testified. Also appearing Ms. Raquel Fox as Supervising Attorney for the Plaintiffs. Other appearances by Don Osborne, Building Inspector, as a witness for the Plaintiff was placed under oath and testified.

The court find that the Statement of Damages needs to be more specific, and the new requested exhibits must be filed and courtesy copies provide the Court before it can come to a just figure.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Damages and Injunctive Relief was filed on Sep-02-2020
Substitute services on both Defendants were filed on Jan-14-2021 and effectuated on Dec-16-2020.
Defaults as to both defendants were filed on Apr-07-2021
Statement of damages as to both Plaintiffs were filed on Mar-11-2021
Memo of Costs were filed on Sep-03-2021
Dismissal of Does (all) was filed on Sep-23-2021
Waiver of Witness as to Plaintiff Michael Conant was filed on Mar-07-2022

This matter is continued to Apr-19-2022. Plaintiff's attorney is to submit documentation/exhibits to the Court by Apr-5th for review. Mr. Scott-Smith is advised to appear at the next hearing and Attorney will be allowed to submit a more recent Declaration by Co- Plaintiff Mr. Conant.

Judge: Murlene Randal; Clerk: R. Sahatjian; Deputy: L. Obtinalla; Court Reporter: Robert Balian Cert. #5220.
